title: Whats the most emotional scene in NGE
display_title: Whats the most emotional scene in NGE
wikitext:
[]: What's the most emotional scene in NGE?             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 24, 2011 8:32 am Hm, I'm starting to like these "question" styled threads. Me, its very close between Rei II and Asuka's death. What gets me all teary about Rei II isn't the fact that she dies, but she dies unhappily (as I observe). Sure, she has realized that she has done the right thing, but she didn't want to. She realizes that she is lonely, and she wants someone to be with. And she got so close to getting that, but instead she gets killed. And yeah, maybe she did come back, but she "didn't". That was someone else. Even Shinji was like, "What happened to her? She isn't... herself.." Oh, but it gets worse. Rei was tortured(?) by Armisael. From being raped (mind... and perhaps through her Eva), to watching Shinji being injured (noticing that it is her face (or more) reaching from the Angel and onto the Angel. It made her feel as if it is her fault that Shinji is hurt), and the fact the she couldn't stop it. She felt helpless , except for the only way she (and we; perhaps there was another way) saw: blowing herself to smithereens. Haha, she exploded, pain and all. Fun way to die, huh? Lets go back to mind-rape part (the part Asuka's death lacks). Armisael explains that it is in pain, the pain that is loneliness. (I think) It points out that Rei and she(?) are feeling the same way, and that they could escape the pain by having each other. But both of them knew that wasn't going to happen. Hell, for as far as we know, the only semi-confirmed reason that the Angels were trying to initiate 3I was to get rid of the lilim, because they could not co-exist with each other. To the point, Rei (whether she realized it or not) was going to be used to bring back the Angels (the Angel tree, [s]we[/s] I haven't figured out what the hell it does. Perhaps it was to bring back the Angels). Thats not really fair, is it? How come Armisael gets to bring back all its friends, but Rei doesn't. Then the horrible truth is revealed: Her only friend is Shinji (And Gendo if you don't consider him as a father-figure to Rei). I have a relatively hard time trying to explain this bit here, so perhaps you could help with that? Now, to Asuka's death. The most ironic thing about it is that usually, determination is what brings you to victory. Yeah.. not here. It was what killed her. Had she not reach out, vowing revenge, she might of actually got it. I don't care how smart those MPE's are, they would of thought she was dead before she moved. She didn't need to die just yet. To add, Asuka was probably at her best at that moment. Her spirits were higher then in any part of the series (and probably combined). She was unbeatable . On the other hand, Rei was literally sitting there. True, she was using all her might and A.T Field to force Armisael out, but she was in better spirits, and I think she could have done better. I think in combat, Asuka was better in combat then Rei (sure, Rei saved her ass, but Asuka was a loner anyways. She was ready to die), yet she died a painful, mortifying death that would of broken anyone (probably why she had to die. I don't think she could of lived with herself after that). Back to the point, she was at the point of winning, but instead of winning, she gets shot down (if you may) by the Lance, eaten alive (basically), only to be stabbed again by multiple Lances, one splitting her arm in half. I guess its a reoccurring in NGE, having Asuka get shot down every time (or not) she was going to win. the And.. drum-roll please.. Shinji was watching. Yeah, maybe Shinji witnessed Rei's death, but it was a huge explosion, with light and dust in his eyes, shielding the horror of Eva-00 being destroyed. Shinji visibly watched Asuka have all the horrible things happen to her. Sure, it doesn't have a mind-rape scene, but she's already had hers (Arael), no need for two (Hell, if she had two, I wonder how she'd turn out). In the end, Asuka wins this (I'm saying this as a R/S shipper, FYI), because she died a painful death, with a false hope that she can't lose, only to die needlessly (with her potential boyfriend watching). But the good part is that she comes back, as her usual self (as far as we know it), to live on to be the awesome person she is/will become (and Rei doesn't, how great). And you, whats the most emotional scene (keep in mind emotional doesn't always mean sad.. but you probably knew that). Picture + Song =Teary eyes

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Sun Apr 24, 2011 8:59 am The hospital scene in End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 24, 2011 9:24 am Well, that'd be very hard a choice, Evangelion is one single emotional rolercoaster, I feel I'm doing X-million other moments an injustice if I pick one... Tough I don't ship S/A, I think the scene before/leading up to the first strangulation in EoE is a powerful moment because you see these two viciously destroying each other... when all they want is to be together, its so heartwrenching! But since I have to pick one, I'd say the prize goes to Kaworu's Death. It's just so evil. Little, depressed Shinji has reached the lowest of the low, everyone who was dear to him is gone, and then, along comes that new kid who totally finds him awesome and wants to be his friend - and then, Shinji has to kill him. After he gave such a ****ing heartwarming speech....

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sun Apr 24, 2011 10:38 am Misato's last moments with Shinji at the elevator.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sun Apr 24, 2011 1:00 pm If I had to pick one scene that I think everyone could appreciate as being highly emotional (as opposed to ones that were only emotional because they related to my own life) I'd have to go with Komm, süßer Tod in EoE. From the moment Shinji grabs Asuka we're treated to visual extravaganza as the entire world essentially comes to an end. Many of our favorite characters are suddenly degraded to tang and are washed away to some other kind of existence. All the while, the song in the background has a bittersweet sort of feel to it, which makes the entire scene all the more strange and powerful. And when it's all done, we're treated to a mellow and beautiful piano piece real life humans begin to walk across the scene. Suddenly, the viewer is able to relate the perils of the animated Evangelion world to those of the one they live in, and realize that, angels and Evas aside, they actually aren't too different.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Apr 24, 2011 1:18 pm Pretty much all of EoE. Though, if I had to pick one, I'd agree that the Komm Susser Tod/Third Impact scene is the most emotional of the series. That, or the rejection of Instrumentality and the return to Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Apr 24, 2011 2:36 pm End of EvaTV, especially when the orchestrated OP theme kicks in. Then Komm Susser Tod. But I wonder if Tsubasa wo Kudasai counts though...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 24, 2011 2:37 pm There's also this old thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1629

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 24, 2011 2:44 pm Old thread is old, and it has quote pyramids. Only foos like quote pyramids.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:13 pm EoE elevator scene > Komm Susser Tod > Asuka mind rape > Asuka's death > Rei II's death For me anyway, more or less. Misato is my favorite character so that scene (i.e elevator) hit me pretty hard, and I think it's overall one of the best scenes in NGE since it's just plain unfiltered raw emotion. Not to mention the testicle crushingly sad rhythm-only version of Thanatos..

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:18 pm The Dummy Plug scene was the first really intense moment of the show for me; I nearly vomited. After that happened I felt like I had an idea of what I was in for, so subsequent events didn't have a huge emotional impact on me and I was able to just enjoy the ride. Episode 26 felt like a great day in church and I watched it three times in a row on my first viewing, so that would probably be second, followed by the shutdown of Instrumentality/Yui's monologue sequence at the end of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:20 pm toe mash wrote: Not to mention the testicle crushingly sad rhythm-only version of Thanatos..

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:26 pm Stryker wrote: I found this version of Thanatos , and oh my rogering god, it is amazing. Listen in.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sun Apr 24, 2011 3:49 pm Stryker wrote: I found this version of Thanatos , and oh my rogering god, it is amazing. Listen in.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 24, 2011 4:03 pm toe mash wrote: When I was in that infamous NGE-mania phase after completing the seriesm, I'd surf youtube for hours checking any OST related to the series. I also happened to stumble upon that particular version of it. It's really great :)

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Apr 24, 2011 4:25 pm It's really hard to pick, but I think the death of Rei II would be at the top of my list.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Apr 24, 2011 4:43 pm Well, for me it would have to be... Third Impact Kaji's death and Misato's reaction Kaworu's death Shinji going berserk Shamshel Asuka's mind rape / Asuka's death The Dummy Plug demolishing Bardiel Shinji choking Asuka (which is exclusive from Third Impact IMO) Rei's death(s)

[]:             Quote Post by Rei Makes You Go Splat » Sun Apr 24, 2011 8:31 pm Stryker wrote: I found this version of Thanatos , and oh my rogering god, it is amazing. Listen in.

[]:             Quote Post by Knuckles » Sun Apr 24, 2011 8:33 pm The elevator scene in EoE for me as well. Not only was it the most raw and honest dialogue in the entire series, it's exactly what Shinji needed to hear. Then there was that kiss... so powerful and real, given the context of the scene. It was such a long time coming, and it was worth the wait. It definitely shook Shinji out of his slump and had him wiping his tears away. Then came the icing on the most-emotional-scene-in-evangelion cake: Shinji sees Misato's blood in his mouth, and realizes he's never going to see her again. He breaks right back down again, even worse than before. Just wow.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Sun Apr 24, 2011 10:57 pm ... No one reacted to my post? Is it because it is a weak troll or because everyone just assumed I really meant it?
